title: Incidental Medical Expense Assistance * Breast Cancer - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Incidental Medical Expense Assistance * Breast Cancer 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Breast Cancer Cancer Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CANCERCARE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited financial assistance for cancer-related transportation, child care and home care expenses. Funding depends on the sources of support received at any given time. If no funding is available, professional oncology social workers will refer to other financial assistance resources. Does not cover basic living expenses such as rent, mortgages, utility payments or food. 485 Madison Avenue New York, NY 10022 Eligibility: Have a diagnosis of cancer confirmed by an oncology health care provider, be in active treatment for cancer, live in the US or Puerto Rico, and meet other eligibility guidelines based on the Federal Poverty Limit. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-3pm, Friday 7am-2pm Email (800) 813-4673 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PINK LEMONADE PROJECT PINK LEMONADE PROJECT FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance to breast cancer patients who are experiencing hardship during treatment or recovery. Additional programs include support groups, peer mentorship, retreats, in-person and online events, screening support, cancer education for community health workers, and programs for those living with metastatic breast cancer. 1207 Washington Street Suite 125 Vancouver, WA 98660 Eligibility: There are no gender restrictions. To qualify, a person must: -Be receiving breast cancer treatment -Be living in or receiving care in Oregon -Be experiencing financial hardship during treatment Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (360) 952-3814 5 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SUSAN G KOMEN KOMEN FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance for individuals currently undergoing breast cancer treatment or living with metastatic breast cancer. Awards may be used for treatment-related expenses, such as: housing, utilities, transportation, child and elder care, food, side effect management medication, oral treatment medication, durable medical equipment, lymphedema care and supplies, palliative care, and home health care. 13770 Noel Road Suite 801889 Dallas, TX 75380 Eligibility: Applicants must be in treatment for breast cancer diagnosed in the past 24 months or living with metastatic breast cancer. Must also have a current annual household income at or below 300% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) and not have received an award from this program in the past 12 months. Hours: Monday-Thursday 6am-4pm, Friday 6am-3pm Email (877) 465-6636 Get Directions Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
